# Testing Standards
<!-- Generated by handover v1.2.1 -->

## Framework

- Use pytest exclusively; no unittest-style tests
- All packages must have a `tests/` directory from Phase 0
- Use `conftest.py` fixtures for shared setup: sample repo paths, in-memory SQLite, mock language analyzer

## Coverage expectations

- `contracts`: 100% — all schemas and interfaces must be tested for valid/invalid inputs
- `graph-index`: 90%+ — symbol extraction and edge detection for all 4 languages
- `ranking`: 90%+ — all mode rankers, token budget enforcer, explain output
- `runtime`: 90%+ — all parsers against real sample output files
- `memory`: 85%+ — write/search/stale detection
- `storage-sqlite`: 85%+ — repository CRUD and FTS5 queries
- `core`: 80%+ — use-case coordinators
- `adapters-*`: 80%+ — output structure and required fields
- `mcp-server`: integration tests for all 8 tools; schema validation on every response
- `cli`: smoke tests for every command; assert exit codes

## Test types

- **Unit tests:** pure functions, parsers, rankers — no filesystem or SQLite
- **Integration tests:** indexing sample repos, MCP tool calls end-to-end, adapter file generation
- **Benchmark tests:** run the benchmark harness as a test and assert reduction_pct > 0 to prevent regressions

## Sample fixtures

- Maintain 4 minimal sample repos in `packages/benchmark/sample_repos/`: Python, Java, .NET, multi-repo
- Parser tests must use real sample stack trace / test report files in `tests/fixtures/`
- Never mock the Tree-sitter parser itself; test against real source files

## CI expectations

- All tests must pass before any merge
- Coverage report generated on every run
- Benchmark harness runs as part of CI on the 4 sample repos and reports summary
